narratives.full_description:
Sound as a Way of Thinking: Emotion, Structure, Atmosphere. What does it really mean to design sound?

The Role of Project Thinking 
 The Role of Project Thinking in the Creative Process 
 Aram Hovhannisyan 
 
 How do ideas take shape? Where does inspiration meet structure? In this open lecture, renowned composer Aram Hovhannisyan delves into the power of project thinking as a quiet force within the creative process. 
 
 Designed for music makers of all genres and backgrounds, the talk will explore how structure and planning can serve not suppress artistic expression. Through stories and reflections, Aram will share how composers and creators can use project thinking to navigate complexity, remain focused, and breathe life into their boldest visions. 
 Whether you’re just starting out or deep into your artistic path, this lecture invites you to rethink how creativity and clarity can coexist and even thrive together. 
 Come curious. Leave inspired. 
 
 Date : May 17 
 Time : 17:00 
 Location : The State Philarmonia of Armenia, Yerevan 
 
 
 Partners 
 Partners 
 
 Aram Hovhannisyan, Composer 
 
 Born in 1984 in Yerevan, Aram Hovhannisyan studied composition with Levon Chaushyan and later with Michael Jarrell in Geneva, earning his MA in 2011. A laureate of international competitions, his works have been performed at major festivals and by renowned ensembles such as Klangforum Wien and Collegium Novum Zürich. 
 
 He co-founded the “Quarter Tone” organization and has directed the Crossroads Festival and Assonance ensemble since 2017. Returning to Armenia in 2018, he became Vice-Rector at the Yerevan State Conservatory, where he now also teaches composition. 
 
 The State Philharmonia of Armenia 
 https://babajanyanhall.org/en/ 
 
The State Philharmonia of Armenia has been  established in 1930. Throughout its activity, Philharmonia has played a significant role in preserving and developing Armenian musical culture. International educational and cognitive projects, concerts, festivals, competitions, master classes, presentations are organised by the institution. Philharmonia is also engaged in the production of scientific-cognitive films and publishing. The mission of Philharmonia is to contribute to the development of the country's cultural sector by presenting the best works of Armenian and world art.

---

Basic Sound Synthesis 
 Basic Sound Synthesis 
 Andriesh Gandrabur 
 
 How is the timbre shaped? How do synthesizers craft sound, and why is noise more than just chaos? 
 
 hosq  presents Synthesis and Electronic Music — a series of lectures and workshops for musicians, instrumentalists, and composers exploring the art and science of sound. The first session, Basic Sound Synthesis, will be led by hosq head curator Andriesh Gandrabur 
 
 “We’ll explore the fundamentals of synthesis — oscillators, filters, and samplers — examining their role in electronic, computer, and contemporary music. The discussion will cover acoustics, psychoacoustics, and music psychology, connecting synthesis to voice leading and overtone perception.” 
 
 
 Partners 
 Partners 
 
 Andriesh Gandrabur 
 
 Composer, sound designer, and educator working at the intersection of sound art, media, and performance. Founder of the Sound Art and Sound Design program at HSE School of Design. At hosq , he guides programs that expand sound-based research into broader interdisciplinary contexts. 
 
 The State Philharmonia of Armenia 
 https://babajanyanhall.org/en/ 
 
The State Philharmonia of Armenia has been  established in 1930. Throughout its activity, Philharmonia has played a significant role in preserving and developing Armenian musical culture. International educational and cognitive projects, concerts, festivals, competitions, master classes, presentations are organised by the institution. Philharmonia is also engaged in the production of scientific-cognitive films and publishing. The mission of Philharmonia is to contribute to the development of the country's cultural sector by presenting the best works of Armenian and world art.
narratives.post_effect_continuation_narrative:
https://conservatory.am 
 
Yerevan State Conservatory (YSC) after Komitas was founded in 1921 as a music studio and re-founded two years later as a higher musical education institution. 
 
 At the beginning of the 20th century, the Armenian professional performing culture was shaped. It was represented by the graduates of Russian and European conservatories. Violinist Hovhanness Nalbandyan, (St. Petersburg Conservatory and Berlin Musikhochschule), singer M.Akimova (Leipzig Conservatory), musicologist M.Aghayan (St. Petersburg and Moscow Conservatories), singer A.Shahmuradyan (Conservatoire de Paris) etc. 
 
 The founder and first rector of conservatory was famous Armenian composer and widely educated musician and public figure Romanos Melikyan (Saints-Petersburg Conservatory graduate)
